These broken windows are nothing compared to the beautiful cracks I will spray across this ruins.
The flood washed away the fences, so now I have a clear path to tag every wall.
Everything is already falling apart, which makes it the perfect canvas for my biggest piece yet.
I will use the chaos of the storm to leave my mark where no one can stop me.
The sirens are loud, but the sound of my paint cans shaking is the only thing I hear.
I will smash these telescope lenses to pieces before the stars change again.
This fancy observatory wall is the perfect place to spray my name in neon.
I want to see if these expensive control panels spark when I hit them.
Let's see how many of these fancy glass domes I can crack tonight.
I'm going to rip the wiring out of this machine while the sky glows.
I will spray my name across this fence if you try to move it.
Your new wall is just another surface for me to smash with this hammer.
I do not care where the line is when I am breaking your windows.
I can paint a giant red X right over your disputed property markers tonight.
Go ahead and argue about the dirt while I kick in your front door.
This glowing ring looks like the perfect place to spray some neon paint.
I wonder if these strange shimmering walls will scratch if I use this brick.
It would be a shame to leave this pristine portal without a giant tag.
I bet the people on the other side would hate seeing their gateway defaced.
I am going to see how many cracks I can put in this weird surface.
I will carve my mark deep into these ancient trees so all know I passed through.
These pristine stone formations are far too clean and need my jagged symbols across them.
I can smash these delicate forest shrines into rubble as I push toward the clearing.
It feels right to rip through these thick vines and leave a trail of destruction behind.
I will spray my colors across every smooth rock face I find in this green hell.
I have scratched my name into every smooth stone while waiting for a sound.
The silence is too heavy so I will smash these pots to hear something.
My hands ache to break the stillness by tearing down these old hanging banners.
I will carve deep lines into the walls until the quiet finally breaks away.
There is nothing to do here but ruin these carvings to pass the time.
You cannot stop me from splashing this paint across your pristine white walls.
I will smash every single one of these windows before you can grab me.
Go ahead and call the police, but the graffiti is already permanent now.
This whole building looks too perfect, so I am going to tear it down.
Your threats do not scare me because I have already ruined your favorite statue.
This shiny glass case is far too sturdy, but I will crack it.
I love the sound of this precious painting ripping under my dirty nails.
All these fancy gold carvings look much better when they are smashed flat.
I am going to spray my name across every inch of this statue.
It feels good to break something that someone else spent years making perfect.
I will smash these water pipes just to see the precious liquid waste away.
There is too much order here, so I will break the fuel pumps now.
It feels good to scratch deep lines into the walls of the grain silo.
I am going to overturn the food crates and scatter everything across the dirt.
These medicine cabinets are too clean, so I will shatter every single glass vial.
